title: Supernova
puzzle: Why do some stars end their lives in spectacular explosions?
insight: A supernova happens when a massive star collapses or a white dwarf becomes unstable, releasing enormous energy and scattering material into space.
key_ideas: Supernovae can briefly outshine entire galaxies.
They create and spread many heavy elements.
Their shockwaves can trigger new star formation.
mind_blower: The iron in your blood and the calcium in your bones were shaped by stars that died violently long before Earth existed.
next_curiosity: If exploding stars seed the universe with raw materials, what do they leave behind after the blast?
narration_text: Why do some stars end their lives in spectacular explosions? A supernova happens when a massive star collapses or a white dwarf becomes unstable, releasing enormous energy and scattering material into space. Supernovae can briefly outshine entire galaxies. They create and spread many heavy elements. Their shockwaves can trigger new star formation. The iron in your blood and the calcium in your bones were shaped by stars that died violently long before Earth existed. If exploding stars seed the universe with raw materials, what do they leave behind after the blast?
